articles.title: Rajasthan: Heatwave to persist for two days, followed by rain and storms - ANI News
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Rajasthan
articles.full_text:
Jaipur (Rajasthan) [India], June 11 (ANI): Heatwave conditions will continue in Western Rajasthan for two more days. According to the Meteorological Department, the impact of severe heat will persist, especially in the border districts, for the next 48 hours. However, weather patterns are expected to shift starting June 11, with an increase in thunderstorms, lightning, and rainfall activities in several parts of the state.
Radheshyam Sharma, Director of the Jaipur Meteorological Centre, stated that the state's highest maximum temperature was recorded at 46.4°C in Sri Ganganagar over the last 24 hours. Most regions in Western Rajasthan are currently seeing temperatures between 44°C and 46°C.
He added that from June 11, the weather will begin to change by the afternoon in the Bikaner division, the Shekhawati region, and districts within the Jaipur and Bharatpur divisions. These areas can expect strong thunderstorms, gusty winds, and moderate to heavy rainfall.
These weather conditions, including storms and rain, are expected to continue through June 12, 13, and 14. The impact of the Western Disturbance is expected to be most significant on June 13, with wind speeds potentially reaching 60-70 km/h in the Bikaner, Shekhawati, Jaipur, and Bharatpur regions. In light of this, the Meteorological Department has issued an Orange Alert for these specific areas.
Regarding the monsoon, the Director noted that it has already advanced into parts of Southern and Northeastern India. It is still too early for the monsoon to reach Rajasthan, and the department is closely monitoring the situation. Further updates will be provided as conditions become favourable. He emphasised that the current heatwave is expected to subside as the Western Disturbance activates, bringing relief through increased rainfall and storm activity.
A fresh Western Disturbance is expected to affect the Western Himalayan region starting June 11. This system is set to bring a transition to the weather across much of North India. According to the IMD, isolated to scattered rainfall is expected across Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand from June 9-14. Broader rainfall activity is also likely to extend to Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, and various parts of Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan.
This atmospheric shift is expected to be accompanied by more severe weather phenomena. The IMD has issued warnings for thunder squalls with wind speeds reaching 50-60 kmph, along with gusty winds and lightning, across states like Rajasthan, Haryana, Delhi, and Uttar Pradesh between June 11 and 12. (ANI)
Rajasthan: Heatwave to persist for two days, followed by rain and storms
ANI | Updated: Jun 11, 2026 11:31 IST

articles.full_text:
Heatwave conditions will persist in Western Rajasthan for the next two days, with severe heat especially affecting border districts, the Meteorological Department has confirmed. From June 11, however, weather patterns are expected to shift, bringing more thunderstorms, lightning, and rainfall across several parts of the state.
Radheshyam Sharma, Director of the Jaipur Meteorological Centre, reported that Sri Ganganagar recorded the state’s highest maximum temperature at 46.4°C over the past 24 hours, while most areas of Western Rajasthan experienced temperatures between 44°C and 46°C.
He added that by the afternoon of June 11, weather changes will commence in the Bikaner division, the Shekhawati region, and districts in the Jaipur and Bharatpur divisions. These areas can expect strong thunderstorms, gusty winds, and moderate to heavy rainfall.
Such weather conditions, including storms and rain, are forecast to continue through June 12, 13, and 14. The impact of the Western Disturbance will be most pronounced on June 13, with wind speeds reaching 60-70 km/h in Bikaner, Shekhawati, Jaipur, and Bharatpur. The Meteorological Department has issued an Orange Alert for these regions.
Regarding the monsoon, Sharma noted that it has advanced into parts of Southern and Northeastern India but has not yet reached Rajasthan. The department is monitoring the situation closely and will provide further updates as conditions allow. He emphasised that the current heatwave is expected to wane as the Western Disturbance becomes active, bringing relief through increased rainfall and storm activity.
A new Western Disturbance is expected to impact the Western Himalayan region beginning June 11, marking a transition in weather across much of North India. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts isolated to scattered rainfall in Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand from June 9 to 14. Broader rainfall is also anticipated in Punjab, Haryana, Delhi, and parts of Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan.
The atmospheric shift will coincide with more severe weather events. The IMD has issued warnings for thunder squalls with wind speeds of 50-60 km/h, accompanied by gusty winds and lightning, across Rajasthan, Haryana, Delhi, and Uttar Pradesh between June 11 and 12.
